records.ety: [OE. wivere a serpent, OF. wivre, guivre, F. givre, guivre, wiver, from L. vipera; probably influenced by OHG. wipera, from the Latin. See Viper, and cf. Weever.]

defs: A fabulous two-legged, winged creature, like a cockatrice, but having the head of a dragon, and without spurs.
quotes: The jargon of heraldry, its griffins, its mold warps, its wiverns, and its dragons.
